sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Difficulty adjusting in dim environments and distorted vision.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports difficulty adjusting in dim environments, specifically when eating in low-light settings like the basement of the new Italian restaurant. He notes that lines appear distorted, particularly in his door frame, and this has been occurring for approximately three to four months. He has tried hypnosis and various lozenges and chewing gum to quit smoking, but has been unsuccessful. He reports taking his blood pressure medication sporadically and states it's been stored in the cupboard for a while. The patient also mentions his wife and he enjoy eating out almost every night.
Findings
The otoscopic examination reveals posterior chamber intraocular lenses in both eyes. The cornea is clear in both eyes. The right eye shows no peripheral retinal detachment or vitreous hemorrhage. The left eye shows no peripheral retinal detachment or vitreous hemorrhage. Fluorescein angiography reveals a focal area of leakage in the right eye consistent with neurovascular age-related macular degeneration. Drusen deposits are noted under the retinal pigment epithelium. Subretinal fluid and exudate accumulation are present.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The diagnostic test and eye exams are consistent with neurovascular age-related macular degeneration, also known as wet macular degeneration. This condition is advanced to a stage that typically leads to more severe vision loss.
Plan
I want to give you an injection of lucent once a month into the vitreous portion of the eye after numbing the eye so you don't feel any pain. Patients report no pain at all for the actual injection. I'd like to schedule you for that injection today. I'll have the nurse come in and get you prepped. ,
